celebration: THE GHOST LETTERS — kn, wr, mb — letters that are written but never heard. A narrative gift right before graduation: the class learns that some letters keep silent, and the long-promised 'know' (held since Week 45) finally lands: k-n-ow.

songs.title: The Ghost Letters
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(shh… shh… shh…)
Some letters are… silent.

[Hook]
k is a ghost! (shh — silent k!)
knee… knot… know!
The k is there… but you can't hear it!
(knee! knot! know!)

[Verse 2]
w is a ghost! (shh — silent w!)
write… wren… wrist!
The w is there… but you can't hear it!
(write! wren! wrist!)

[Verse 3]
b is a ghost! (shh — silent b!)
lamb… thumb… comb… climb!
The b is there… but you can't hear it!
(lamb! thumb! climb!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! No ghost letters!
(shh… shh… shh…)

[Outro]
silent k… silent w… silent b…
written… but never heard. (shh…)
know! (we know it now!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: The spookiest, most fun week for the dark-trap style — silent letters ARE ghosts. Say the WORDS normally (knee = /niː/, lamb = /læm/) and whisper the ghost letter with an 'shh' — never sound the k, w, or b. The gag is that the letter is written but haunts silently. The long-held 'know' lands here (k-n-ow). Potato bridge intact.

songs.title: The Lamb Can Climb
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
The lamb can climb!
The lamb can climb!
Where is the lamb? (where? where?)
Up on the rocks!

[Verse 2]
The lamb sees a wren.
The wren can not climb.
The lamb has a knot! (a knot!)
The lamb kneels down.

[Verse 3]
Now the lamb can climb!
Up, up, to the top!
The lamb and the wren know the way!
(climb! climb! climb!)

[Outro]
The lamb… the top… (shhh…)
What is on the lamb?!
(POTATO!)
The lamb can climb. (all the way up!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'The lamb can climb.' — a little lamb (Sheep's own kin) climbing rocks, helped by a silent wren. Every silent letter word sung normally — climb ends in a silent b, wren begins with a silent w, knot with a silent k, know too. The choir whispers the ghost letters. Whisper the outro; the potato rode up on the lamb's back.

spreads.artNote: A small white lamb — Sheep's little kin — in the spotlight, looking up at something tall. 'Lamb' ends in a silent b.
The lamb picking its way up a dark rocky slope, careful and determined. 'Climb' — silent b again.
A tiny brown wren perched on a rock above the lamb, tilting its head. 'Wren' — silent w at the start.
The wren hopping helplessly at a sheer bit of rock, one wing folded — it cannot get up the smooth face.
A rope with a big knot caught around the lamb's leg, snagged on the rock. 'Knot' — silent k. Trouble.
The lamb folding its front legs to kneel and work the knot loose with its teeth. 'Kneels' — silent k. Patience.
Free of the knot, the lamb springing up the rocks with new energy, the wren riding on its back.

spreads.text: The lamb and the wren know the way.
spreads.artNote: Lamb and wren together at the summit rock, gazing out. 'Know' — the word held since Week 45, silent k, landed at last. Sheep watches proudly from below.
Riding on the lamb's woolly back all the way to the top, wearing a tiny climbing harness: a single potato. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. The wren peers at it.

sentences: The lamb and the wren know the way.
extras: THE GHOST-LETTER STORY: frame the whole week as a mystery — some letters are written but stay silent, like ghosts. Three families: silent k before n (knee, knot, know, knife), silent w before r (write, wren, wrist, wrong), silent b after m (lamb, thumb, comb, climb). Say each word normally, then hunt the ghost — the child circles the letter you cannot hear.
🎉 'know' LANDS: back in Week 45 the class met 'snow' and 'grow' and was told 'know' would have to wait for a silent letter. Today it arrives — k-n-ow. Point back to the promise.

Pattern-card tracing (pattern mode): trace kn, wr, mb with the SILENT letter greyed and haloed — the child traces it and says nothing, the same delicious silent-e joke from magic-e, now for ghost letters.
Moveable alphabet: build kn-ee, wr-i-te, l-a-mb, th-u-mb, then hide the ghost letter behind a finger and read the word — proof it was silent all along.

teacherFocus: The ghost-letter week — the most fun the dark-trap theatre will ever have. Some letters are written but never sounded, and children LOVE a rule that says 'don't say it.' Three families: silent k before n (knee, knot, know), silent w before r (write, wren, wrist), silent b after m (lamb, thumb, climb). Always say the word normally, then hunt the letter you couldn't hear. Pattern-card tracing greys the silent letter with a halo — trace it, say nothing. 🎉 Point back to Week 45: 'know' was promised the day the class met 'snow'; today it lands (k-n-ow). One week from graduation.
